item.title: Report Claims Samsung Might Post Its First-Ever Mobile Division Loss This Year, Blaming RAM Crisis 摘要
item.description:
Samsung's mobile division (MX) may post its first-ever annual operating loss, according to reports from Korea. TM Roh, head of the division, has expressed concerns about a possible deficit, partly due to a RAM crisis. Historically, Samsung's mobile division has always been profitable. Meanwhile, Apple continues to dominate industry profits, capturing an estimated 75-90% of profit share with only 20% of unit sales. If Samsung posts a loss, Apple could theoretically capture 100% of phone industry profits.
标签: Samsung, Apple, mobile, profit, loss, smartphone, industry, RAM crisis, market share, finance

item.title: ★ The New York Times Printed the Wrong Crossword Grid Last Sunday, and I Find That Timing Serendipitous 摘要
item.description:
The New York Times printed a Sunday crossword puzzle with a grid that didn't match the clues, the first such error in 84 years. Solvers expressed frustration and self-doubt, with some assuming the mistake was intentional. The article contrasts the permanence of print errors with the fixability of digital ones, drawing a parallel to hardware vs. software mindsets. It suggests that incoming Apple CEO John Ternus, with a hardware background, may lead Apple to focus on perfection and deliberate pace rather than rushing into AI trends.
标签: New York Times, crossword, print error, journalism, hardware vs software, Apple, John Ternus, AI hype, perfectionism, media

item.title: DF Paraphernalia: Last Call for This Round of T-Shirts and Hoodies 摘要
item.description:
John Gruber reflects on the 20th anniversary of going full-time with Daring Fireball, noting that his passion for the site remains unchanged since his 2006 announcement. He promotes his current t-shirt and hoodie sale, urging readers to purchase before it ends. Gruber acknowledges longtime and new readers, including those born after he started writing full-time.
标签: Daring Fireball, anniversary, blogging, merchandise, John Gruber, tech writing, personal reflection, sale
